entry.title: The Cheese Monkeys
entry.summary:
by Chip Kidd

✮✮✮✮☆

This is a book I equate with the essence of the best parts if high school. It got passed all around (as a great book should be), and it felt like pretty much required reading for the teenage, slightly self-conscious art students we were.

I let a …
entry.content:
by Chip Kidd

✮✮✮✮☆

This is a book I equate with the essence of the best parts if high school. It got passed all around (as a great book should be), and it felt like pretty much required reading for the teenage, slightly self-conscious art students we were.

I let a couple of English majors take a crack at it recently, and they brought up some good points about its flaws. Still a classic, and still worthy of reading if only for the gloriousness that is Winter Sorbeck.

(Fair warning: there is one, infamous part that gets downright weird.)

entry.title: A Tale of Time City
entry.content:
by Diana Wynne Jones

no star rating

entry.title: Anansi Boys
entry.summary:
by Neil Gaiman

✮☆☆☆☆

I last read this right after it came out (when I was in high school) and I have no intention of reading it again, but I don't have good memories of this book. I was a huge fan of Good Omens at the time, but this was …
entry.content:
by Neil Gaiman

✮☆☆☆☆

I last read this right after it came out (when I was in high school) and I have no intention of reading it again, but I don't have good memories of this book. I was a huge fan of Good Omens at the time, but this was the one that convinced me that while Gaiman has good ideas sometimes, he's a lousy writer.

entry.title: Bitten (Women of the Otherworld, #1)
entry.summary:
by Kelley Armstrong

✮✮☆☆☆

bechdelfail

This used to be my favorite werewolf duology, which says more about the competitors to that title than it does about this book. I don't even remember what happens in the book, but I remember that I 100% loathed Clayton.

I enjoy Armstrong's other entries in …
entry.content:
by Kelley Armstrong

✮✮☆☆☆

bechdelfail

This used to be my favorite werewolf duology, which says more about the competitors to that title than it does about this book. I don't even remember what happens in the book, but I remember that I 100% loathed Clayton.

I enjoy Armstrong's other entries in the Women of the Otherworld series, but I do not do not do not like her werewolves. I have plenty of guilty pleasure books, but from this day forward any werewolf novel that doesn't pass the werewolf Bechdel test is dead to me. AKA, if you can't have lesbian werewolves, why would you even bother??

entry.title: Blood and Chocolate
entry.summary:
by Annette Curtis Klause

✮✮☆☆☆

I read this a few times when I was in high school. The absolute best thing about it is the way the relationship between 16 year old goths Vivian (werewolf) and Aidan (human) goes sour. If Vivian didn't end up dating a disgusting 24 year old …
entry.content:
by Annette Curtis Klause

✮✮☆☆☆

I read this a few times when I was in high school. The absolute best thing about it is the way the relationship between 16 year old goths Vivian (werewolf) and Aidan (human) goes sour. If Vivian didn't end up dating a disgusting 24 year old who'd also slept with her mother, it would be about ten times better.

by Ursula Vernon

entry.title: Drowned Ammet (The Dalemark Quartet, #2)
entry.content:
by Diana Wynne Jones

✮✮✮✮☆

I didn't remember this being such a page turner, but oops I stayed up til 2:45am because I needed to know what happened next.

by Terry Pratchett

no star rating

entry.title: Power of Three
entry.summary:
by Diana Wynne Jones

✮✮✮✮☆

DWJ is so, so, so good at emotional development and family dynamics. The only sour note was a minor but persistent thread of fatphobia. Brenda even gets development and a heroic arc of her own, but for some reason (I guess: being published almost 50 years …
entry.content:
by Diana Wynne Jones

✮✮✮✮☆

DWJ is so, so, so good at emotional development and family dynamics. The only sour note was a minor but persistent thread of fatphobia. Brenda even gets development and a heroic arc of her own, but for some reason (I guess: being published almost 50 years ago) we can't have that without the narrative constantly making vicious little jabs about her every time I thought it had finally been dropped.

entry.title: Shadow of the Hegemon (The Shadow Series, #2)
entry.summary:
by Orson Scott Card

no star rating

The only thing that I remember about this, fifteen years on, is the part where a message is encoded in the dithering of a graphic of a dragon that then gets spread as a meme across the internet. They don't use the words …
entry.content:
by Orson Scott Card

no star rating

The only thing that I remember about this, fifteen years on, is the part where a message is encoded in the dithering of a graphic of a dragon that then gets spread as a meme across the internet. They don't use the words meme or internet, obvs.

This should not be considered a recommendation.

entry.title: The Case of the Missing Marquess (Enola Holmes, #1)
entry.content:
by Nancy Springer

no star rating

Sort of forgot I'd read this til I started watching the movie on Netflix the other night. Not bad, but if it was catering to my tastes there would be more crossdressing.
